description: Wildlife experiences — safari tours, birdwatching, marine wildlife encounters, nocturnal tours. Highly seasonal, international market, and dependent on guides with specialist knowledge.
key_characteristics: Highly seasonal demand — tied to migration, breeding, or weather patterns
Guide availability and specialist knowledge is the primary constraint
Small group sizes for quality experience
Pre-arrival communication is important — what to bring, what to expect
pain_points: Small group sizes mean individual booking management overhead is proportionally high
International guests researching and booking months in advance need a smooth direct booking path
Pre-arrival communication (gear lists, expectations, meeting points) done manually
Guide allocation across multiple tours is managed outside the booking system
Seasonal peaks and lulls make forward booking visibility important for guide scheduling
general_discovery_questions: How far out are your bookings typically coming in — months in advance or last-minute?
What does your pre-arrival communication look like — do you send guests info about what to bring, what to expect?
How do you manage guide allocation across your different tours?
Are most of your guests international visitors or domestic?
fareharbor_strengths: International payment options and multi-currency support
